digest.component_or_area: JWKS/OIDC JWT verifier on the MCP auth seam
propositions: Add a JWKSTokenVerifier selected by the jwt token in mcp.auth_strategies that validates JWTs against a JWKS endpoint for generic OIDC providers.

The verifier validates signature with pinned algorithms, iss, aud==auth_resource_server_url, exp/iat/nbf with bounded leeway, and optionally header typ when auth_jwt_expected_typ is set, mapping scope/scp to scopes.
It rejects alg:none and refuses to start when a symmetric algorithm (HS256/HS384/HS512) is configured with a JWKS source, blocking the RS256/HS256 confusion attack.
JWKS fetch is SSRF-safe: HTTPS-only jwks_uri fixed from trusted config, private/link-local hosts rejected by default unless auth_jwt_allow_private_jwks_host=true, with a bounded timeout.

Blocking JWKS fetches are offloaded via asyncio.to_thread and the unknown-kid refetch cooldown is made atomic under an asyncio.Lock so an attacker flooding random kids triggers at most one fetch per cooldown window.
A JWKS-fetch failure raises so the composite denies, while a definitively-invalid token returns None; the last-refetch timestamp updates on every fetch attempt to prevent flood-guard bypass.
